description: Standard operational dashboard shipped with jetstream (contrib/grafana). Expects Prometheus to scrape the debug listener's /metrics endpoint.

panels.title: Phase
panels.description: Ingestion lifecycle phase. 0 is reserved for a process that never reached the dispatch loop.

panels.title: Build
panels.description: Version label from jetstream_build_info.

panels.title: Uptime
panels.description: Uptime of the most recently started selected instance — shortest uptime surfaces recent restarts.

panels.title: Firehose events/s
panels.description: Upstream subscribeRepos events decoded per second.

panels.title: Appended events/s
panels.description: Events durably appended to the active segment per second. During backfill this is dominated by repo downloads.

panels.title: Upstream freshness
panels.description: Seconds since steady-state live ingest last saw an upstream event. No data until steady state.

panels.title: Data dir free
panels.description: Free bytes on the filesystem holding the data directory.

panels.title: Subscribers
panels.description: Connected /subscribe websocket clients.

panels.title: Firehose intake
panels.description: Upstream subscribeRepos decode results. Unknown events are archival holes a jetstream upgrade would fix.

panels.title: Relay sequence gaps
panels.description: Forward gaps in the relay's seq stream: upstream data loss, not local decode trouble. Should be flat at zero.

panels.title: Connection health
panels.description: Websocket reconnects and op=-1 error frames from the relay. Error-frame series appear only after the first occurrence.

panels.title: Replay & verify drops
panels.description: Deliberate steady-state drops (relay seq replays, stale resyncs) vs verify-queue overflow, which is permanent archival loss.

panels.title: Verifier failures
panels.description: Verifier rejections by error class. A sustained 'other' rate means a new class needs categorizing.

panels.title: Append throughput
panels.description: Writer.Append successes and failures. Append errors indicate writer trouble and should be flat at zero.

panels.title: Dropped at validation gate
panels.description: Upstream records dropped at the ingest validation gate. Spec-invalid input vs unrepresentable vs upstream omissions.

panels.title: Blocks & rotations
panels.description: zstd block fsync rate and segment rotations (rotations shown as count per interval — they are rare).

panels.title: Active segment size
panels.description: Compressed bytes written since the active segment's header. Saw-tooth resets on rotation.

panels.title: Segment seal latency
panels.description: End-to-end Writer.Seal duration (flush + footer + header pwrite + fsyncs), successful seals only.

panels.title: Readable log
panels.description: In-memory readable log serving hot /subscribe reads. Pinned overrun means retention budget exceeded by undurable bytes.

panels.title: Backfill progress
panels.description: Repos completed this Run vs DIDs discovered via listRepos. Approximate: discovery keeps running during backfill.

panels.title: Repos completed
panels.description: Repos the engine reported complete in the current Run.

panels.title: Repos failed (run)
panels.description: DIDs that exhausted their retry budget within the Run.

panels.title: Completion queue depth
panels.description: Repo completions waiting for a writer durable metadata batch.

panels.title: Repo throughput
panels.description: listRepos discovery vs getRepo completion vs terminal failure.

panels.title: HandleRepo latency
panels.description: Per-repo download+append duration, successful repos only.

panels.title: Completion durability wait
panels.description: Time a repo completion waited between being queued and becoming durable. A rising tail is a durability backlog.

panels.title: Steady-state failed-repo retry
panels.description: Retry loop that drains repos which failed initial backfill.

panels.title: Subscribers by compression
panels.description: Connected /subscribe clients by negotiated wire compression.

panels.title: Events sent to subscribers
panels.description: JSON frames written to clients, plus events removed by per-subscriber filters before encoding.

panels.title: Egress bytes
panels.description: Bytes written to /subscribe clients (post-compression for zstd) and archive getBlock downloads. sent/encoded ratio approximates the compression ratio.

panels.title: Subscriber drops & errors
panels.description: Per-subscriber trouble. Encode errors and adversarial drops should be flat at zero.

panels.title: Cursor requests by mode
panels.description: New /subscribe connections by cursor resolution outcome.

panels.title: Hot vs cold reads
panels.description: ReadFrom calls served from the in-memory readable log vs falling through to the cold disk reader.

panels.title: HTTP requests
panels.description: Public-listener HTTP request rate by handler and status.

panels.title: API latency (p99)
panels.description: 99th percentile latency of the public API surfaces.

panels.title: getBlock requests
panels.description: Archive getBlock requests by outcome. Series appear after the first request.

panels.title: Compaction passes
panels.description: Delete/update compaction passes by result, plus passes triggered early by the tombstone cap.

panels.title: Rows dropped & tombstones collected
panels.description: Compaction work: rows physically dropped from rewritten segments and tombstones collected by kind.

panels.title: Tombstone set
panels.description: Entries in the live in-memory tombstone set. Drops to zero after each compaction pass.

panels.title: Compaction watermark lag
panels.description: witnessed_at lag between the sealed segment tip and the compaction watermark (header granularity).

panels.title: Compaction rewrite I/O
panels.description: Segment-file bytes rewritten by compaction.

panels.title: Sealed segments
panels.description: Sealed segments tracked by the manifest, and compaction rewrite activity per interval.

panels.title: Block index cache
panels.description: Manifest BlockIndex lookups served from resident metadata. No data while there are no cold lookups.

panels.title: Store ops
panels.description: Pebble metadata-store operations by op and outcome. Any error series is a crash-loud-worthy signal.

panels.title: Integrity signals (expect zero)
panels.description: Data-integrity error counters from every subsystem. All of these should be flat at zero; investigate any movement.

panels.title: Phase timeline
panels.description: 1=bootstrap, 2=merging, 3=steady state. Transitions are also marked as dashboard annotations.

panels.title: Merge rev filter
panels.description: live_segments/ events surviving the rev filter into the steady-state segments vs already covered by backfill.

panels.title: Merge progress
panels.description: Run-once merge counters, shown as raw totals.

panels.title: Cutover state durations
panels.description: Total wall-clock seconds spent in each cutover state across this process lifetime.

panels.title: Phase transitions
panels.description: Phase transitions per interval; normally each fires exactly once per data-dir lifetime.

panels.title: Import phase
panels.description: Current timestamp-import phase.

panels.title: Import rows
panels.description: Timestamp-import pipeline throughput across phases.

panels.title: Import rows rejected
panels.description: CSV rows rejected during parse, by reason.

panels.title: Import rewrite activity
panels.description: Phase C segment rewrites per interval.

panels.title: Import rewrite I/O
panels.description: On-disk segment bytes rewritten by import.

panels.title: Import jobs
panels.description: Terminal import jobs per interval by result.

panels.title: CPU
panels.description: Process CPU usage in cores vs the scheduler limit.

panels.title: Memory
panels.description: Resident set vs Go heap. GOMEMLIMIT is hidden when unset.

panels.title: Goroutines & threads
panels.description: A monotonic goroutine climb is a leak.

panels.title: GC pauses
panels.description: Stop-the-world pause durations from the Go GC summary.

panels.title: Process network I/O
panels.description: Process-level network throughput (procfs; Linux only).

title: Jetstream
